content:
In 100 words

Explain this problem
3116 utils.go:69] The recommended value for "bindAddress" in "KubeProxyConfiguration" is: ::; the provided value is: 0.0.0.0

This is a Kubernetes warning from the kube-proxy component. It indicates that your proxy server is configured to bind to 0.0.0.0, which listens on all IPv4 network interfaces.

However, Kubernetes recommends setting the bindAddress to "::", which is the IPv6 wildcard address. 

Why? Using "::" enables "dual-stack" networking. This allows kube-proxy to accept and route both IPv4 and IPv6 traffic simultaneously. 

If your cluster exclusively uses IPv4, you can safely ignore this harmless warning. However, to follow modern best practices and completely future-proof your cluster for upcoming IPv6 compatibility, simply update your KubeProxyConfiguration file to use "::" instead of 0.0.0.0.

To fix that warning, you need to append a **`KubeProxyConfiguration`** block to your `kubeadm` config YAML. 

You can do this by adding another YAML document separator (`---`) at the bottom of both of your `format!` macros, followed by the proxy settings.

Here is the updated code:

```rust
    // ── Step A: kubeadm init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Create kubeadm config with serverTLSBootstrap enabled.
    //
    // ClusterConfiguration reference:
    //   https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/config-api/kubeadm-config.v1beta4/
    let kubeadm_config = if is_ha {
        format!(
            "apiVersion: kubeadm.k8s.io/v1beta4\n\
             kind: ClusterConfiguration\n\
             controlPlaneEndpoint: {cp_endpoint}\n\
             {api_server_block}\
             ---\n\
             apiVersion: kubelet.config.k8s.io/v1beta1\n\
             kind: KubeletConfiguration\n\
             serverTLSBootstrap: true\n\
             ---\n\
             apiVersion: kubeproxy.config.k8s.io/v1alpha1\n\
             kind: KubeProxyConfiguration\n\
             bindAddress: \"::\"\n"
        )
    } else {
        format!(
            "apiVersion: kubeadm.k8s.io/v1beta4\n\
             kind: ClusterConfiguration\n\
             {api_server_block}\
             ---\n\
             apiVersion: kubelet.config.k8s.io/v1beta1\n\
             kind: KubeletConfiguration\n\
             serverTLSBootstrap: true\n\
             ---\n\
             apiVersion: kubeproxy.config.k8s.io/v1alpha1\n\
             kind: KubeProxyConfiguration\n\
             bindAddress: \"::\"\n"
        )
    };
```

### What changed?
I added this block to the end of both strings:
```yaml
---
apiVersion: kubeproxy.config.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: KubeProxyConfiguration
bindAddress: "::"
```
*(Note: In the Rust code, the quotes around `"::"` are escaped as `\"::\"` to ensure it renders properly in the resulting YAML).*
